title: Persistent /scratch via WASI
tagline: In Pie inferlets get a WASI filesystem capability. The engine pre-opens /scratch as a writable, per-user persistent directory. User code calls std::fs directly, no Redis, no sidecar service.
question: Same question, asked twice. Answer once, cache to /scratch, return the cached value the second time.

naive.label: Naive
naive.sublabel: no inline storage — every call re-asks the model

events.value: Speed of light is a constant, approximately 299,792,458 meters per second.
generated, about 156 ms — answer goes to the client; nothing persists
call 2  (same question, no place to remember)
Speed of light is a constant, approximately 299,792,458 meters per second.
about 229 ms total  •  2 model calls  •  no persistence

pie.label: Pie
pie.sublabel: memoize via /scratch/answers.json — second call is a file read

events.value: Speed of light is a constant, approximately 299,792,458 meters per second.
hit ✓ — answered from disk, no model call
Speed of light is a constant, approximately 299,792,458 meters per second.
about 73 ms total  •  1 model call  •  3.1× total, call 2 served from disk

naive.value:
# To memoize: stand up Redis (or a DB) in front of the LLM.
key=$(echo "$Q" | sha1sum | cut -d' ' -f1)
hit=$(redis-cli GET "answer:$key")
if [ -n "$hit" ]; then echo "$hit"; exit; fi

resp=$(curl /v1/responses \
  -d "{\"model\":\"qwen3-0.6b\", \"input\":\"$Q\"}")

redis-cli SET "answer:$key" "$resp"
echo "$resp"

runCommand:
# enable filesystem in ~/.pie/config.toml: allow_fs = true
pie run demo-filesystem -- --question "What is the speed of light?"
